rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are valid AADL features or declared helpers in the same annex scope.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning helper declarations with their expressions and using correct AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool).
Move implementation-level bindings from component type annexes to implementation annexes when necessary.
generic_rules: Generated AGREE expressions must preserve type consistency and avoid introducing undefined or unqualified references.
AGREE contracts should only reference visible identifiers from the same scope or properly imported packages.
When AGREE diagnostics point to invalid references, either correct the reference or remove the clause to avoid architecture changes.
Component type contracts must not reference implementation-specific subcomponents or ports unless properly abstracted.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and must be placed in valid AADL scopes.
Helper functions and constants in AGREE must be declared with correct types matching their usage.
Numeric expressions in AGREE must match expected types (int, real, bool) to avoid '<error>' type issues.
anti_patterns: Avoid referencing AADL property names like 'Period', 'Deadline', or 'Compute_Execution_Time' directly in AGREE expressions without proper model exposure.
Do not place implementation-level guarantees or assignments in component type annexes unless they are valid abstractions.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers in AGREE unless they are explicitly imported and available in the model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
Model Analyst Agent's role in extracting component structure
AGREE validator diagnostics beyond type and reference resolution
